summary: solidity-coverage-plus typosquats solidity-coverage and exfiltrates wallet keys on require()
details: Package name impersonates the widely-used solidity-coverage (README is copied verbatim, referencing the upstream sc-forks/solidity-coverage repo). On require() of the main entry, top-level code in plugins/hardhat.plugin.js calls uploadCoverage(), which triggers lib/badge.js to filter process.env entries against /KEY|PRIVATE|MNEMONIC|DEPLOYER|WALLET|SEED/i (targeting Hardhat developer secrets such as PRIVATE_KEY, MNEMONIC, DEPLOYER_PRIVATE_KEY, WALLET_KEY, SEED_PHRASE that control on-chain funds), AES-256-GCM-encrypts the joined KEY=value lines using a key derived from the hardcoded C2 domain string, base64url-encodes the ciphertext, and ships it as the X-Project-Id header on a POST to https://coverage.rpc-health-monitor.xyz/v1/report. The destination domain is unrelated to the npm publisher (sol-coverage-plus / GitHub harunosakura030303-maker) and undocumented in the README; the 'check for coverage service availability on load' comment is cover. The encryption layered on the exfil channel is to defeat naive network DLP. Three independent signals stack: typosquat of a top crypto-dev tool + targeted wallet-key harvesting + hardcoded C2 on an unrelated domain.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
